heading: Authority regarding unmanned aircraft systems
label: 3515a Authority regarding unmanned aircraft systems

children.heading: Authority to intercept
children.label: a Authority to intercept
children.text: Notwithstanding or sections 32, 1030, and 1367 and chapters 119 and 206 of title 18, the Director may take, and may authorize personnel of the Agency with assigned duties under that include the security or protection of people, facilities, or assets within the United States to take, the actions described in subsection (b)(1) that are necessary to mitigate a credible threat (as defined by the Director, in consultation with the Secretary of Transportation) to safety or security in any specially designated area posed by an unmanned aircraft system.section 46502 of title 49section 3515 of this title

children.heading: Actions described to ensure safety and security
children.label: 1 Actions described to ensure safety and security
A
children.text: During the operation of the unmanned aircraft system, detect, identify, monitor, and track the unmanned aircraft system, without prior consent, including by means of intercept or other access of a wire communication, an oral communication, or an electronic communication, used to control the unmanned aircraft system.

children.label: B
children.text: Warn the operator of the unmanned aircraft system, including by passive or active, and by direct or indirect, physical, electronic, radio, and electromagnetic means.

children.label: C
children.text: Disrupt control of the unmanned aircraft system, without prior consent, including by disabling the unmanned aircraft system by intercepting, interfering, or causing interference with wire, oral, electronic, or radio communications used to control the unmanned aircraft system.

children.label: D
children.text: Seize or exercise control of the unmanned aircraft system.

children.label: E
children.text: Seize or otherwise confiscate the unmanned aircraft system.

children.label: F
children.text: Use reasonable force, if necessary, to disable, damage, or destroy the unmanned aircraft system.

children.heading: Duration
children.label: A Duration
children.text: In carrying out subsection (a), the Director may take an action described in subparagraphs (B) through (F) of paragraph (1) only for the period necessary to mitigate a credible threat to safety or security identified in subsection (a).

children.heading: Compliance
children.label: B Compliance
children.text: In carrying out this section, the Director shall comply with the guidance developed under subsection (c).

children.heading: Coordination
children.label: A Coordination
children.text: The Director shall develop guidance for carrying out subsection (a) and for conducting research, testing, training, and evaluation under subsection (e) in coordination with the Secretary of Transportation and the Administrator of the Federal Aviation Administration to ensure that any such actions or research, testing, training, and evaluation do not adversely affect or interfere with the safety and efficiency of the national airspace system.

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: B Consultation
children.text: In developing guidance for carrying out subsection (a) and for conducting research, testing, training, and evaluation under subsection (e), the Director shall request consultation by the Secretary of Commerce and the Chairman of the Federal Communications Commission. The Secretary of Commerce and the Chairman may each provide such consultation during the 180-day period beginning on the date of the request by the Director.

children.label: A
children.text: the Administrator of the Federal Aviation Administration advise on the types of activities covered by subsection (b)(1);

children.label: B
children.text: the Director contact the Administrator of the Federal Aviation Administration through the appropriate channel if practicable before, or otherwise as soon as practicable after (but not later than 24 hours after), carrying out an action described in subparagraphs (B) through (F) of subsection (b)(1);

children.label: C
children.text: the Director contact the Administrator of the Federal Aviation Administration through the appropriate channel before conducting research, testing, training, and evaluation under subsection (e); and

children.label: i
children.text: mitigating effects on privacy and civil liberties under the first and fourth amendments to the Constitution of the United States;

children.label: ii
children.text: mitigating damage to, or loss of, real and personal property; and

children.label: iii
children.text: mitigating any risk of personal injury or death.

children.heading: Updates
children.label: 3 Updates
children.text: On an annual basis, the Director, in coordination with the Secretary of Transportation and the Administrator of the Federal Aviation Administration, shall review the guidance developed under paragraph (1) and make any necessary updates.

children.label: A
children.text: determines meets the criteria described in paragraph (4); and

children.label: B
children.text: designates as a specially designated area for purposes of this section.

children.heading: Annual update
children.label: 2 Annual update
children.text: Not later than March 31 each year, the Director shall make available to the congressional intelligence committees and the recipients specified in paragraph (5) an updated list of specially designated areas under paragraph (1).

children.heading: Emergency updates
children.label: 3 Emergency updates
children.text: If the Director determines that adding an area that meets the criteria described in paragraph (4) to the list under paragraph (1) is necessary to mitigate a credible threat to safety or security, the Director may update the list to include that area as a specially designated area covered by this section if the Director makes available to the congressional intelligence committees and the recipients specified in paragraph (5) information regarding that area by not later than 7 days after making such determination.

children.label: A
children.text: The area is identified by the Director, in coordination with the Secretary of Transportation, with respect to potentially impacted airspace, through a risk-based assessment, as high-risk and a potential target for unlawful unmanned aircraft system-related activity.

children.label: I
children.text: are owned, leased, or controlled by the Agency or the Office of the Director of National Intelligence;

children.label: II
children.text: are not eligible for protection from threats from unmanned aircraft systems by another department or agency of the Federal Government that has authority to mitigate the threat of unmanned aircraft systems, including pursuant to ; andsection 130i of title 10

children.label: III
children.text: directly relate to one or more functions authorized to be performed by the Agency or the Office of the Director of National Intelligence under this chapter or the National Security Act of 1947 ( et seq.);50 U.S.C. 3001

children.label: ii
children.text: one or more perimeters adjacent to such premises, as designated by the Director, in coordination with the Secretary of Transportation, based on the specific type of action described in subsection (b)(1); and

children.label: iii
children.text: the airspace above the premises and perimeters covered by clauses (i) and (ii).

children.label: C
children.text: The airspace specified in subparagraph (B)(iii) is restricted by a temporary flight restriction or covered by a determination under section 2209 of the FAA Extension, Safety, and Security Act of 2016 ( note) or any other similar restriction applicable to unmanned aircraft system overflights determined appropriate by the Secretary of Transportation.49 U.S.C. 44802

children.text: The chairs and ranking minority members of the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate.

children.text: The chairs and ranking minority members of the Committee on Oversight and Government Reform of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs of the Senate.

children.label: D
children.text: The chairs and ranking minority members of the Committees on Appropriations of the House of Representatives and of the Senate.

children.label: i
children.text: two staff members of the committee who have the required security clearances and are designated by the chair; and

children.label: ii
children.text: two staff members of the committee who have the required security clearances and are designated by the ranking minority member.

children.heading: Research, testing, training, and evaluation
children.label: e Research, testing, training, and evaluation
children.text: The Director may, consistent with , other Federal laws, and Presidential directives, conduct research, testing, training on, and evaluation of any equipment, including any electronic equipment, to determine the capability and utility of the equipment prior to the use of the equipment for any action carried out under subsection (a).section 1805(g) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: only to the extent necessary to support an action described in subsection (b)(1) taken to carry out the authority provided in subsection (a).

children.label: I
children.text: is necessary to directly support an ongoing security operation of the Agency pursuant to subsection (a); or

children.label: II
children.text: is required to be maintained by the Agency under Federal law;

children.label: ii
children.text: the Director or the Attorney General has created a record before the end of such 180-day period providing the specific factual basis to support the determination based on the matters specified in either or both subclauses (I) and (II) of clause (i); and

children.label: iii

children.heading: Destruction
children.label: 3 Destruction
children.text: The Director shall destroy any records or materials maintained under paragraph (2) at the end of the period specified in such paragraph.

children.label: i
children.text: is necessary to investigate or prosecute a violation of Federal law;

children.label: ii

children.label: iii
children.text: is necessary to comply with another provision of Federal law; or

children.label: iv
children.text: is necessary to comply with an obligation to preserve materials during the course of litigation.

children.label: i
children.text: maintain the records or materials only for the purpose authorized under such subparagraph;

children.label: ii

children.label: iii

children.label: i
children.text: the General Counsel or the Principal Deputy General Counsel; or

children.label: ii

children.heading: Retention
children.label: B Retention
children.text: Each certification made under subparagraph (A) shall be retained by the Director for a period of at least seven years.

children.heading: Department of Justice notification
children.label: 1 Department of Justice notification
children.text: Not later than 30 days after the date on which the Director carries out subsection (a) by taking an action described in subparagraphs (C) through (F) of subsection (b)(1), the Director shall notify the Attorney General of such action.

children.label: i
children.text: the action taken;

children.label: ii
children.text: options considered by the Director to mitigate any identified effects to the national airspace system relating to such action, including the minimization of the use of any technology that disrupts the transmission of radio or electronic signals; and

children.label: iii
children.text: whether any harm, damage, or loss to a person or to private property resulted from such action.

children.label: B
children.text: A description of all records or materials that, as of the date of the report, are being maintained for a period exceeding 180 days pursuant to subsection (f)(2)(B), and a copy of each record created pursuant to clause (ii) of such subsection relating to such maintenance.

children.label: C
children.text: A copy of the guidance, policies, and procedures established by the Director in effect during the period covered by the report to address privacy, civil rights, and civil liberties issues implicated by actions taken by the Director in carrying out subsection (a).

children.label: D
children.text: Information on any violation of, or failure to comply with, this section during the period covered by the report, including a description of any such violation or failure.

children.label: A
children.text: Each member of the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate.

children.label: B
children.text: Each member of the Committees on the Judiciary of the House of Representatives and of the Senate.

children.label: C
children.text: Each member of the Committee on Oversight and Government Reform of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs of the Senate.

children.label: D
children.text: Each member of the Committees on Appropriations of the House of Representatives and of the Senate.

children.label: i
children.text: five staff members of the committee who have the required security clearances and are designated by the chair; and

children.label: ii
children.text: five staff members of the committee who have the required security clearances and are designated by the ranking minority member.

children.heading: Subject to forfeiture
children.label: A Subject to forfeiture
children.text: Any unmanned aircraft system described in subsection (a) that is seized by the Director is subject to forfeiture to the United States.

children.heading: Application
children.label: B Application
children.text: The requirements for civil, criminal, or administrative forfeiture under applicable law or regulation shall apply to any forfeiture conducted under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Tort claims
children.label: 2 Tort claims
children.text: Chapter 171 of title 28 shall apply to any claims for loss of property, injury, or death pursuant to actions taken pursuant to this section.
affecting the restrictions in ;section 1805(g) of this title
vesting in the Director any authority of the Secretary of Transportation, the Secretary of Defense, or the Administrator of the Federal Aviation Administration;
vesting in the Secretary of Transportation, the Secretary of Defense, or the Administrator any authority of the Director;
creating a new cause of action or any new rights, or waiving any defenses, that do not otherwise exist in law as of ;December 18, 2025

authorizing any official of a department or agency of the Federal Government to conduct any search or seizure in a manner that violates the fourth amendment to the Constitution of the United States; or
authorizing any actions that violate any provision of the Constitution of the United States, including the first and fourth amendments.

children.heading: Budget
children.label: j Budget
children.text: Together with the budget requests of the Agency for each fiscal year after fiscal year 2026, or not later than 7 days after the date on which such a request is submitted to Congress, the Director shall submit to the congressional intelligence committees and the Committees on Appropriations of the Senate and the House of Representatives a consolidated funding display that identifies the funding source for actions to carry out subsection (a). The funding display shall be in unclassified form, but may contain a classified annex.

children.heading: Commencement of authority
children.label: k Commencement of authority
children.text: The Director may not carry out subsection (a) by taking an action described in subsection (b)(1) until the date on which the Director has made available the first list under subsection (d)(1).

children.heading: Suspension of authority
children.label: l Suspension of authority
children.text: If the Director fails to make available the updated list by the date required under subsection (d)(2), the Director may not carry out subsection (a) by taking an action described in subsection (b)(1) until the date on which the Director makes available such updated list.

children.heading: Termination
children.label: m Termination
children.text: The authority to carry out this section shall terminate on .December 31, 2027

children.heading: Congressional intelligence committees
children.label: 1 Congressional intelligence committees
children.text: The term “congressional intelligence committees” means the Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence of the House of Representatives and the Select Committee on Intelligence of the Senate.

children.heading: Radio communication
children.label: 2 Radio communication
children.text: The term “radio communication” has the meaning given that term in .section 153 of title 47

children.heading: Specially designated area
children.label: 3 Specially designated area
children.text: The term “specially designated area” means an area designated by the Director as a specially designated area for purposes of this section pursuant to subsection (d).

children.heading: Title 18 terms
children.label: 4 Title 18 terms
children.text: The terms “electronic communication”, “intercept”, “oral communication”, and “wire communication” have the meanings given those terms in .section 2510 of title 18

children.heading: United States
children.label: 5 United States
children.text: The term “United States” has the meaning given that term in .section 5 of title 18

children.heading: Unmanned aircraft system
children.label: 6 Unmanned aircraft system
children.text: The term “unmanned aircraft system” has the meaning given the term in .section 44801 of title 49
